---
title: "Visualizing Plastics: Waste Composition and Environmental Cost"
author: "Nathan Lee, Noah Nayve, Ryan Ekdahl, Taiga Sobajima"
format:
  dashboard:
    scrolling: true
    orientation: rows
    theme: flatly
    logo: "logo.png"
    nav-buttons:
      - icon: envelope
        href: mailto:nlee174@calpoly.edu
        aria-label: Nathan Lee
      - icon: envelope
        href: mailto:nnayve@calpoly.edu
        aria-label: Noah Nayve
      - icon: envelope
        href: mailto:rekdahl@calpoly.edu
        aria-label: Ryan Ekdahl
      - icon: envelope
        href: mailto:tsobajim@calpoly.edu
        aria-label: Taiga Sobajima
server: shiny
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(tidyverse)
library(glue)
library(gt)
library(plotly)
library(scales)
library(ggridges)
library(broom)
library(ggrepel)
library(rvest)
library(httr)
library(geodata)
library(leaflet)
library(sf)

```

```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| context: setup
#| message: false

population_plastics <- read_csv("data/population_plastics.csv") |>
  mutate(country = case_when(
    country == "United States Of America" ~ "United States of America",
    country == "Taiwan_ Republic Of China (Roc)" ~ "Taiwan",
    TRUE ~ country
  ))
```

```{css}
.card-toolbar {
  padding: 4px 8px;
  min-height: 0;
}

.card-toolbar .selectize-input {
  padding: 2px 6px;
  min-height: 0;
  font-size: 12px;
}
```

```{r}
#| label: prep
#| message: false
#| warning: false

summarize_plastic_and_ecology <- function(country_name, data = population_plastics) {
  if (!(country_name %in% data$country)) stop(paste("Country not found:", country_name))
  country_stats <- data |>
    filter(country == country_name, parent_company == "Grand Total", grand_total > 0) |>
    summarize(
      country = country_name,
      avg_pet = mean(pet / grand_total, na.rm = TRUE),
      avg_hdpe = mean(hdpe / grand_total, na.rm = TRUE),
      avg_ldpe = mean(ldpe / grand_total, na.rm = TRUE),
      avg_pp = mean(pp / grand_total, na.rm = TRUE),
      avg_ps = mean(ps / grand_total, na.rm = TRUE),
      avg_pvc = mean(pvc / grand_total, na.rm = TRUE),
      avg_o = mean(o / grand_total, na.rm = TRUE),
      avg_forested = mean(forested_area, na.rm = TRUE),
      avg_threatened = mean(threatened_species, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  leading_plastics <- country_stats |>
    pivot_longer(cols = !c(country, avg_forested, avg_threatened),
                 names_to = "plastic_type", values_to = "avg_value") |>
    slice_max(order_by = avg_value, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
    mutate(plastic_type = str_remove(plastic_type, "avg_"),
           plastic_type = str_to_upper(plastic_type),
           plastic_type = if_else(plastic_type == "O", "Other", plastic_type)) |>
    pull(plastic_type)
  country_stats |> mutate(leading_plastic = leading_plastics)
}

# precompute all needed data
all_countries_rq2 <- population_plastics |>
  filter(parent_company == "Grand Total", grand_total > 0,
         !is.na(forested_area), !is.na(threatened_species)) |>
  distinct(country) |> pull(country)

plastic_ecology_summary_table <- map(all_countries_rq2, summarize_plastic_and_ecology) |>
  bind_rows()

top3_plastics <- population_plastics |>
  filter(parent_company == "Grand Total") |>
  summarize(across(c(pet, hdpe, ldpe, pp, ps, pvc), ~ sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "plastic", values_to = "total") |>
  slice_max(total, n = 3) |> pull(plastic)

gt_style <- function(gt_obj) {
  gt_obj |>
    tab_style(style = cell_fill(color = "#457B9D"), locations = cells_column_labels()) |>
    tab_style(style = cell_text(color = "white", weight = "bold"), locations = cells_column_labels()) |>
    tab_style(style = cell_fill(color = "#1D3557"), locations = cells_column_spanners()) |>
    tab_style(style = cell_text(color = "white", weight = "bold"), locations = cells_column_spanners())
}
```

# Main

## Row 1 {height="450px"}

### Bubble Plot {width="70%" .tabset}

#### Overview

![](https://trashhero.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/75166929_1337270093146099_2568898103847419904_o.jpg)

<p style="color: gray; font-size: 0.9em; font-style: italic;">
Volunteers participate in a global plastic waste cleanup and brand audit. Image source: Break Free From Plastic, “Branded Vol. II: Identifying the World’s Top Corporate Plastic Polluters,” via Trash Hero World: https://trashhero.org/worlds-top-polluters-revealed-by-break-free-from-plastics-2019-brand-audit/.
</p>

Every year, thousands of volunteers around the world collect plastic waste as part of the Break Free From Plastic (BFFP) movement, a global initiative working to stop plastic pollution at its source and hold companies accountable for the waste they produce. The data they gather not only quantifies the scale of plastic pollution, but also reveals how geography shapes the types of waste found across different regions. Using audit data from 2019–2020 across a range of countries, we investigated whether country-level environmental indicators, including forest coverage and threatened-species count, are associated with which types of plastic dominate a country’s waste stream.

Throughout this dashboard, acronyms will be used to identify plastic types. Here are the following acronyms corresponding to each plastic type: PET - polyester, HDPE - high density polyethylene, LDPE - low density polyethylene, PP - polypropylene, PS - polystyrene, PVC - polyvinyl chloride.

That said, this dataset has some important limitations and potential inconsistencies to consider. The data depends on where volunteers are located, meaning it is more likely to be collected in accessible areas, which can lead to over- or under- representation in some regions and introduce bias. Due to the nature of self-reported data, there is no way for us to verify the accuracy, and there may also be variability in how the plastic is classified and reported to BFFP despite clear definitions. Since our analysis spans both 2019 and 2020, not all countries have data for both years, meaning averages for some countries reflect only a single year. Furthermore, some countries did not record data for certain plastic types; since there are entries with zero, these missing values couldn't be assumed to mean zero and were instead excluded from proportion calculations.

#### Bubble Plots

```{r}
#| content: card-toolbar

selectInput("selected_year", "Select Year:",
            choices = sort(unique(population_plastics$year)),
            selected = 2019)

selectInput("selected_y", "Select Y Axis:",
            choices = c("Forest Coverage" = "avg_forested",
                        "Threatened Species" = "avg_threatened"),
            selected = "avg_forested")
```

```{r}
plotlyOutput("bubble_plot", height = "600px")
```

```{r}
#| context: server

bubble_data_reactive <- reactive({
  req(input$selected_year, input$selected_y)
  
  population_plastics |>
    filter(
      year == input$selected_year,
      grand_total > 0
    ) |>
    group_by(country) |>
    summarize(
      total_plastic = sum(grand_total, na.rm = TRUE),
      avg_forested = mean(forested_area, na.rm = TRUE),
      avg_threatened = mean(threatened_species, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(.data[[input$selected_y]]))
})

output$bubble_plot <- renderPlotly({
  data <- bubble_data_reactive()
  y_var <- input$selected_y
  y_label <- if (y_var == "avg_forested") "Forest Coverage (%)" else "Threatened Species Count"
  
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = total_plastic, y = .data[[y_var]], 
                         size = total_plastic,
                         label = country,
                         text = paste0("Country: ", country,
                                      "<br>Total Plastic: ", scales::comma(total_plastic),
                                      "<br>", y_label, ": ", round(.data[[y_var]], 1)))) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "#457B9D") +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 12)) +
    scale_x_log10(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Plastic Count vs", y_label, "—", input$selected_year),
      x = "Total Plastic Collected (log scale)",
      y = y_label
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
          legend.position = "none")
  
  ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text") |>
  layout(margin = list(l = 50, r = 50, t = 50, b = 50))
})
```

### General {width="30%"}

#### Value Boxes

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Countries Analyzed"

list(
  icon = "globe",
  color = "#D3D3D3",
  value = n_distinct(all_countries_rq2)
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Most Common Plastic Collected"

list(
  icon  = "recycle",
  color = "#D3D3D3",
  value = "PET"
)
```

## Row 2

### Density Ridge {width="50%"}

```{r}
#| content: card-toolbar

selectizeInput("selected_plastics", "Select Plastics:",
               choices = c("PET" = "pet",
                           "HDPE" = "hdpe",
                           "LDPE" = "ldpe",
                           "PP" = "pp",
                           "PS" = "ps",
                           "PVC" = "pvc"),
               selected = top3_plastics,
               multiple = TRUE,
               options = list(plugins = list("remove_button"),
                              placeholder = "Click to add a plastic..."))
```

```{r}
plotOutput("ridge_plot", height = "500px")
```

```{r}
#| context: server

ridge_data_reactive <- reactive({
  req(input$selected_plastics)

  population_plastics |>
    filter(parent_company == "Grand Total",
           grand_total > 0,
           !is.na(threatened_species)
           ) |>
    pivot_longer(cols = c(pet, hdpe, ldpe, pp, ps, pvc),
                 names_to = "plastic_type",
                 values_to = "count") |>
    filter(count > 0,
           plastic_type %in% input$selected_plastics) |>
    mutate(plastic_type = factor(toupper(plastic_type),
                                 levels = toupper(input$selected_plastics))
           )
})

output$ridge_plot <- renderPlot({
  data <- ridge_data_reactive()
  n_plastics <- length(unique(data$plastic_type))
  ridge_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("#E8A0A0", "#8B1A1A"))(n_plastics)

  ggplot(data, aes(x = threatened_species,
                   y = plastic_type,
                   fill = plastic_type,
                   weight = count)
         ) +
    geom_density_ridges(scale = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_x_log10(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = ridge_colors) +
    labs(title = "Threatened Species Counts Across Selected Plastic Types",
         subtitle = "Each country weighted by quantity of plastic collected",
         x = "Threatened Species Count (log scale)", y = "") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
          legend.position = "none")
})
```

### Bar Charts {width="50%"}

::: {.panel-tabset}
#### Low Coverage

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Bar chart showing bottom 5 forest coverage countries with plastic waste breakdown"
#| label: bottom 5 countries bar chart with plastic waste breakdown

bottom5 <- plastic_ecology_summary_table |>
  mutate(across(everything(),
                ~replace_na(., 0))
         ) |>
  filter(!is.na(avg_forested)) |>
  slice_min(avg_forested, n = 5)

bottom5_forest <- ggplot(bottom5, aes(x = reorder(country, avg_forested),
                                      y = avg_forested,
                                      text = glue("PET: {round(avg_pet * 100, 2)}%
                                      HDPE: {round(avg_hdpe * 100, 2)}%
                                      LDPE: {round(avg_ldpe * 100, 2)}%
                                      PP: {round(avg_pp * 100, 2)}%
                                      PS: {round(avg_ps * 100, 2)}%
                                      PVC: {round(avg_pvc * 100, 2)}%
                                      Other: {round(avg_o * 100, 2)}%"))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           fill = "#F3D4D5") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(y = "Forest Coverage (%)",
       x = "",
       title = "Bottom 5 Forest Coverage Countries") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplotly(bottom5_forest, tooltip = "text")
```

#### Medium Coverage

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Bar chart showing medium 5 forest coverage countries with plastic waste breakdown"
#| label: medium 5 countries bar chart with plastic waste breakdown

medium5 <- plastic_ecology_summary_table |>
  mutate(across(everything(),
                ~replace_na(., 0))
         ) |>
  filter(!is.na(avg_forested),
         avg_forested >= 19) |>
  slice_min(avg_forested, n = 5)

medium5_forest <- ggplot(medium5, aes(x = reorder(country, avg_forested),
                                      y = avg_forested,
                                      text = glue("PET: {round(avg_pet * 100, 2)}%
                                      HDPE: {round(avg_hdpe * 100, 2)}%
                                      LDPE: {round(avg_ldpe * 100, 2)}%
                                      PP: {round(avg_pp * 100, 2)}%
                                      PS: {round(avg_ps * 100, 2)}%
                                      PVC: {round(avg_pvc * 100, 2)}%
                                      Other: {round(avg_o * 100, 2)}%"))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           fill = "#F3D4D5") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(y = "Forest Coverage (%)",
       x = "",
       title = "Medium 5 Forest Coverage Countries") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplotly(medium5_forest, tooltip = "text")
```

#### High Coverage

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Bar chart showing top 5 forest coverage countries with plastic waste breakdown"
#| label: top 5 countries bar chart with plastic waste breakdown

high5 <- plastic_ecology_summary_table |>
  mutate(across(everything(),
                ~replace_na(., 0))
         ) |>
  filter(!is.na(avg_forested),
         avg_forested >= 36) |>
  slice_max(avg_forested, n = 5)

high5_forest <- ggplot(high5, aes(x = reorder(country, avg_forested),
                                      y = avg_forested,
                                      text = glue("PET: {round(avg_pet * 100, 2)}%
                                      HDPE: {round(avg_hdpe * 100, 2)}%
                                      LDPE: {round(avg_ldpe * 100, 2)}%
                                      PP: {round(avg_pp * 100, 2)}%
                                      PS: {round(avg_ps * 100, 2)}%
                                      PVC: {round(avg_pvc * 100, 2)}%
                                      Other: {round(avg_o * 100, 2)}%"))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",
           fill = "#F3D4D5") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(y = "Forest Coverage (%)",
       x = "",
       title = "High 5 Forest Coverage Countries") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplotly(high5_forest, tooltip = "text")
```
:::

# India

```{r}
#| label: getting India data

URL <- "https://www.pib.gov.in/PressReleasePage.aspx?PRID=1943210&reg=3&lang=2"

# used AI here to help get url, basically disguises so we can fetch the data
page <- GET(URL, user_agent("Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64)"))

tables_india <- content(page, "text") |>
  read_html() |>
  html_table(fill = TRUE)

plastic_raw <- tables_india[[2]]

plastic_india <- plastic_raw |>
  select(state = 2, waste_2017_18 = 4, waste_2018_19 = 5) |>
  slice(-1, -2) |>
  mutate(waste_2017_18 = as.numeric(waste_2017_18),
         waste_2018_19 = as.numeric(waste_2018_19)) |>
  mutate(state = recode(state,
    "Jammu & Kashmir"                          = "Jammu and Kashmir",
    "Madhya  Pradesh"                          = "Madhya Pradesh",
    "Andaman and Nicobar Islands"              = "Andaman & Nicobar",
    "Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu" = "Dadra and Nagar Haveli",
    "Delhi NCR"                                = "NCT of Delhi"
  ))

if (!file.exists("data/india_gadm.rds")) {
  india_GADM <- gadm("IND", level = 1, path = "data/") |> st_as_sf()
  saveRDS(india_GADM, "data/india_gadm.rds")
} else {
  india_GADM <- readRDS("data/india_gadm.rds")
}

india_df <- india_GADM |>
  left_join(plastic_india, by = c("NAME_1" = "state"))
```

## Row 1 {height="30%"}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| label: India is country chosen
#| title: "Country Chosen"

list(
  icon = "geo-fill",
  color = "#D3D3D3",
  value = "India"
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| label: number of India threatened species
#| title: "Threatened Species Count"

list(
  icon = "exclamation-triangle",
  color = "#D3D3D3",
  value = "1,118"
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| label: India forested area
#| title: "Average Forested Area"

india_forest <- plastic_ecology_summary_table |>
  filter(country == "India") |>
  pull(avg_forested)

list(
  icon = "tree",
  color = "#D3D3D3",
  value = paste0(india_forest, "%")
)
```

## Row 2 {height="70%"}

### Column 1

::: {.card}
India has a high forested area and threatened species count. Also, they have the most complete and distributed plastic profile in the data.

```{r}
#| label: bar chart for India plastic breakdown
#| fig-height: 7
#| fig-width: 15

just_india <- plastic_ecology_summary_table |>
  filter(country == "India") |>
  select(-country, -avg_forested, -avg_threatened, -leading_plastic) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
               names_to = "plastic_type",
               values_to = "proportion") |>
  mutate(plastic_type = str_remove(plastic_type, "avg_") |> str_to_upper()) |>
  mutate(plastic_type = case_when(plastic_type == "O" ~ "Other",
                                  TRUE ~ plastic_type))

india_colors <- c("PVC"  = "#880e4f",
                  "PS" = "#ad1457",
                  "PP" = "#c2185b",
                  "PET"   = "#e91e63",
                  "Other"   = "#f48fb1",
                  "LDPE" = "#ffb3c6",
                  "HDPE"  = "#fce4ec"
                  )

ggplot(just_india, aes(x = proportion,
                          y = plastic_type,
                          fill = plastic_type)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(proportion * 100, 1), "%")),
            hjust = -0.2,
            size  = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = india_colors) +
  labs(title = "Plastic Type Distribution — India",
       x     = "Proportion",
       y     = ""
       ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 18),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 15),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 25, face = "bold"))
```
:::

### Column 2

```{r}
#| label: map with states and territories

pal <- colorNumeric("RdPu", domain = india_df$waste_2018_19, na.color = "#cccccc")

leaflet(india_df) |>
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) |>
  setView(lng = 90, lat = 22, zoom = 3.5) |>
  addPolygons(fillColor = ~pal(waste_2018_19),
              fillOpacity = 0.8,
              color = "black",
              weight = 1.5,
              label = ~paste0(NAME_1, ": ",
                              ifelse(is.na(waste_2018_19), "No data",
                                     paste(formatC(waste_2018_19, format="f", digits=0, big.mark=","),
                                           "tonnes"))),
              highlightOptions = highlightOptions(weight = 2, fillOpacity = 0.9)
              ) |>
  addLegend(pal = pal,
            values = ~waste_2018_19,
            title    = "Plastic waste (tonnes, 2018-19)",
            position = "bottomright",
            na.label = "No data")
```
